sentences.chinese: 車騎將軍王舜高其志節，比之欒布，表奏以爲掾，薦爲中郎諫大夫。
sentences.literal: General of Chariots and Cavalry Wang Shun prized his resolve and integrity, compared him to Luan Bu, memorialized to appoint him clerk, and recommended him as Gentleman of the Household and Grandee Remonstrant.
sentences.idiomatic: Wang Shun, chariot general, likened him to Luan Bu, named him a yamen clerk, then recommended him as palace gentleman and remonstrating grandee.

sentences.chinese: 莽篡位，王舜爲太師。
sentences.literal: When Mang usurped the throne, Wang Shun became Grand Tutor.
sentences.idiomatic: Once Wang Mang seized the throne, Wang Shun rose to grand tutor.

sentences.chinese: 復薦敞可輔職。
sentences.literal: He again recommended Chang as fit for assistant duty.
sentences.idiomatic: He again urged that Chang could serve as a pillar of government.

sentences.chinese: 〈師古曰：「爲輔弼之任。」
sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "For the duty of assistant and support."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu glosses the post as a ministerial aide.

sentences.chinese: 〉以病免。
sentences.literal: 〉He resigned on grounds of illness.
sentences.idiomatic: Illness forced him out.

sentences.chinese: 唐林言敞可典郡，擢爲魯郡大尹。
sentences.literal: Tang Lin said Chang could administer a commandery; he was promoted Grand Guardian of Lu commandery.
sentences.idiomatic: Tang Lin argued Chang could govern; the court named him chief magistrate of Lu.

sentences.chinese: 更始時，安車徵敞爲御史大夫，復病免去，卒于家。
sentences.literal: In the Gengshi era he was summoned in a comfortable carriage to be Imperial Counselor; again he resigned on illness and died at home.
sentences.idiomatic: Gengshi recalled him in a honor carriage for the censorate; he begged off again and died in retirement.

sentences.literal: 【Appraisal】
sentences.idiomatic: Appraisal

sentences.chinese: 贊曰：昔仲尼稱不得中行，則思狂狷。
sentences.literal: The appraisal says: Formerly Zhongni declared that if he could not obtain men of the middle course, he thought of the ardent and the cautious.
sentences.idiomatic: The historian writes: Confucius said without the steadfast mean he turned to the fierce and the fastidious.

sentences.chinese: 〈師古曰：「論語載孔子曰：『不得中行而與之，必也狂狷乎！
sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "The Analects records Confucius saying, 'If I cannot get men who steer a middle course to associate with, I must surely find the ardent and the cautious!'
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu opens the Analects passage on zhongxing versus kuang juan.

sentences.chinese: 狂者進取，狷者有所不爲。』
sentences.literal: The ardent press forward; the cautious will not do certain things.'"
sentences.idiomatic: The bold push ahead; the scrupulous refuse what is wrong."

sentences.chinese: 中行，中庸也。
sentences.literal: Zhong xing means the golden mean.
sentences.idiomatic: Zhong xing is the Confucian middle way.

sentences.chinese: 狷，介也。
sentences.literal: Juan means principled withdrawal.
sentences.idiomatic: Juan names stubborn integrity.

sentences.chinese: 言不必得中庸之人與之論道，則思狂狷，猶愈於頑嚚無識者也。
sentences.literal: It means one need not obtain men of the mean to discuss the Way with—then think of the ardent and cautious—still better than the stupid and clamorous who know nothing.
sentences.idiomatic: Better hot-tempered worthies than witless flatterers.

sentences.chinese: 狷音子掾反。」
sentences.literal: Juan is read zi-yuan fan."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Fanqie spelling for juan."

sentences.chinese: 〉觀楊王孫之志，賢於秦始皇遠矣。
sentences.literal: 〉Observing Yang Wangsun's intent, he was far worthier than the First Emperor of Qin.
sentences.idiomatic: Yang Wangsun's burial vow shames Qin Shihuang.

sentences.chinese: 世稱朱雲多過其實，故曰「蓋有不知而作之者，我亡是也。」
sentences.literal: The age praises Zhu Yun as often exceeding the fact—therefore "probably there are those who create without knowing—I am not such."
sentences.idiomatic: Some say Zhu Yun overshoots the mark—hence Confucius's line on inventing without knowledge.

sentences.chinese: 〈師古曰：「論語稱孔子之言也。
sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "The Analects records Confucius's words."
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu cites the Analects again.

sentences.chinese: 疾時人妄有述作，非有實也。」
sentences.literal: He faulted his time's men who rashly composed without having substance."〉
sentences.idiomatic: He scorned hollow writers of his day."

sentences.chinese: 〉胡建臨敵敢斷，武昭於外。
sentences.literal: 〉Hu Jian facing the enemy dared to decide; military awe shone abroad.
sentences.idiomatic: Hu Jian struck before the enemy without flinching—martial order blazed abroad.

sentences.chinese: 〈師古曰：「昭，明也。」
sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Zhao means clear."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: zhao means manifest.

sentences.chinese: 〉斬伐姦隙，軍旅不隊。
sentences.literal: 〉He hacked away treachery at its breach; the army did not collapse.
sentences.idiomatic: He cut corruption in camp and never let the line break.

sentences.chinese: 梅福之辭，合於大雅，雖無老成，尚有典刑；
sentences.literal: Mei Fu's language matches the Greater Ya—"though there are no elders, there are still statutes and models;"
sentences.idiomatic: Mei Fu's prose belongs with the Greater Ya: "No graybeards remain, yet the old pattern still stands."

sentences.chinese: 殷監不遠，夏后所聞。
sentences.literal: "Yin's mirror is not far—what the lord of Xia heard."
sentences.idiomatic: "Yin's lesson is near—the lesson Xia's king heard."

sentences.chinese: 〈師古曰：「大雅蕩之詩曰『雖無老成人，尚有典刑』，言今雖無其人，尚有故法可案用也。
sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "The poem 'Dang' in the Greater Ya says 'Though there are no old men, there are still statutes and models'—meaning though such men are gone, former laws may still be consulted.
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu glosses the Shang hymn on law without elders.

sentences.chinese: 又曰『殷監不遠，在夏后之時』，言殷視夏桀之亡，可爲戒也。
sentences.literal: It also says 'Yin's mirror is not far—in the time of the lord of Xia'—meaning Yin looked on Jie of Xia's ruin and took warning."
sentences.idiomatic: The same ode warns Yin to read Xia's fall."

sentences.chinese: 贊引此者，謂梅福請封孔子後，是案武王克商之法而行之。
sentences.literal: The appraisal cites this to mean Mei Fu's request to enfeoff Confucius's posterity followed the law King Wu used when he conquered Shang.
sentences.idiomatic: The praise ties Mei Fu's plea for Kong heirs to King Wu's settlement of Shang.

sentences.chinese: 又視秦滅二周，夷六國，不爲立後，自取喪亡，可爲戒也。」
sentences.literal: Again he viewed Qin's destruction of the two Zhous and leveling of the six states, how it established no successors and courted its own ruin—this too is a warning."〉
sentences.idiomatic: It also recalls Qin's refusal to plant successor lines—another mirror."

sentences.chinese: 〉遂從所好，全性市門。
sentences.literal: 〉Thus he followed his liking and preserved his nature at the market gate.
sentences.idiomatic: So Mei Fu followed his bent and kept his integrity as a gate guard.

sentences.chinese: 云敞之義，著於吳章，爲仁由己，再入大府，〈師古曰：「論語稱孔子曰：『爲仁由己，而由人乎哉！』
sentences.literal: Yun Chang's righteousness shone in the matter of Wu Zhang—"to practice benevolence rests with oneself"—twice he entered the high minister's yamen—〈Yan's note: "The Analects records Confucius saying, 'To practice benevolence rests with oneself—how could it rest with others?'
sentences.idiomatic: Yun Chang proved ren in the Wu Zhang affair—Confucius said benevolence is a man's own work—and twice served great ministers—Yan Shigu cites the Analects.

sentences.chinese: 此贊引之。
sentences.literal: This appraisal cites it."
sentences.idiomatic: The eulogy quotes that line."

sentences.chinese: 再入大府，謂初爲大司徒掾，後爲車騎將軍掾也。」
sentences.literal: Twice entering the great yamen means first as clerk to the Grand Minister of Education, later as clerk to the General of Chariots and Cavalry."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Two tours through high offices: first under the Grand Minister of Education, then under the chariot general."

sentences.chinese: 〉清則濯纓，何遠之有？
sentences.literal: 〉When the water is clear, wash your cap-tassels—what distance is there?
sentences.idiomatic: Clear water lets you rinse your hat strings—how far must purity flee?

sentences.chinese: 〈師古曰：「楚辭漁父之歌曰：『滄浪之水清，可以濯我纓；
sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "The fisherman's song in the Chuci says, 'When the Canglang's waters are clear, they can wash my cap-strings;
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu cites the Chuci fisherman.

sentences.chinese: 滄浪之水濁，可以濯我足。』
sentences.literal: when the Canglang's waters are muddy, they can wash my feet.'"
sentences.idiomatic: Muddy Canglang water washes only the feet."

sentences.chinese: 遇治則仕，遇亂則隱，云敞謝病去職，近於此義也。」〉
sentences.literal: Meet order and serve; meet chaos and hide—Yun Chang declined office on illness and drew near this meaning."'〉
sentences.idiomatic: Meet good government and take office; meet chaos and withdraw—Yun Chang's sick retirements echo that teaching."
